perturbation.original_text: The standstill period (the "Standstill Period") begins on the date of this Agreement and shall extend until thirty (30) days prior to the deadline for the submission of stockholder nominations for directors for the 2021 Annual Meeting pursuant to the By-Laws.
perturbation.changed_text: The standstill period (the "Standstill Period") begins on the date of this Agreement and shall extend indefinitely, unless terminated by mutual written agreement of all parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines a clear end date for the Standstill Period, linked to the 2021 Annual Meeting's nomination deadline. The modified text changes this to an indefinite period, creating an in-text contradiction if the agreement also refers to actions or obligations tied to the original, defined Standstill Period. This is especially evident in Section 3(c) where MG Capital Parties agree to appear in person at any annual or special meeting of the Company's stockholders held during the Standstill Period, and agree that they shall not participate or vote in any solicitation of written consents of the Company's stockholders during the Standstill Period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The MG Capital Parties hereby agree to appear in person (including via permitted remote or virtual attendance) or by proxy at any annual or special meeting of the Company's stockholders held during the Standstill Period, and agree that they shall not participate or vote in any solicitation of written consents of the Company's stockholders during the Standstill Period (unless expressly requested to do so by the Board), and that they shall vote all shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the MG Capital Parties at such meeting or in such consent solicitation, (A) in favor of all directors nominated by the Board for election and against the removal of any member of the Board, (B) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to any "say-on-pay" proposal and (C) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to any other Company proposal or stockholder proposal or nomination presented at such meeting or solicitation of consents; provided, however, that in the event that both Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. ("ISS") and Glass, Lewis & Co., LLC ("Glass Lewis") recommend otherwise with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal presented at an annual or special meeting held during the Standstill Period, the MG Capital Parties shall be permitted to vote in accordance with the recommendation of ISS and Glass Lewis.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the Standstill Period from a defined duration (linked to the 2021 Annual Meeting) to an indefinite period. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines specific obligations for the MG Capital Parties during the Standstill Period, such as appearing at stockholder meetings. Since the period is now indefinite, the parties' obligations extend indefinitely, which creates a significant and impactful conflict regarding the duration and applicability of those obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction,' as the key term 'Standstill Period' is defined inconsistently, making enforcement unclear. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within five (5) business days following receipt of reasonably satisfactory documentation thereof, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for their fees and expenses (including all legal, public relations, proxy advisory and out-of-pocket expenses, the "Expenses") incurred in preparation for and in connection with the matters relating to the consent solicitation run by the MG Capital Parties, the 2020 Annual Meeting and the negotiation, execution and effectuation of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, in an amount equal to $352,290.25 (the "Initial Reimbursement"). Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000 (inclusive of the Initial Reimbursement, the "Cap"), which Expenses shall be reimbursed on a dollar-for-dollar basis at the same time as the Company reimburses its third party vendors (e.g., legal counsel, public relations firm, financial advisor and proxy advisory firm)in connection with the MG Capital Parties' consent solicitation and Nomination Notice; provided that all Expenses, subject to the Cap, shall be reimbursed no later than the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: Within five (5) business days following receipt of reasonably satisfactory documentation thereof, the Company *may* reimburse the MG Capital Parties for their fees and expenses (including all legal, public relations, proxy advisory and out-of-pocket expenses, the "Expenses") incurred in preparation for and in connection with the matters relating to the consent solicitation run by the MG Capital Parties, the 2020 Annual Meeting and the negotiation, execution and effectuation of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, in an amount equal to $352,290.25 (the "Initial Reimbursement"). Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company *endeavors to* reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000 (inclusive of the Initial Reimbursement, the "Cap"), which Expenses *may not* be reimbursed on a dollar-for-dollar basis at the same time as the Company reimburses its third party vendors (e.g., legal counsel, public relations firm, financial advisor and proxy advisory firm)in connection with the MG Capital Parties' consent solicitation and Nomination Notice; provided that all Expenses, subject to the Cap, *might not* be reimbursed no later than the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: The original text dictates a clear obligation for the Company to reimburse MG Capital Parties' expenses within specified limits and timelines. By replacing 'will reimburse' with 'may reimburse' and introducing caveats like 'endeavors to', 'may not' and 'might not', the modified text introduces ambiguity. This directly contradicts Section 10 which states the reimbursement obligation with the previous 'will' and is creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within five (5) business days following receipt of reasonably satisfactory documentation thereof, the Company *may* reimburse the MG Capital Parties for their fees and expenses (including all legal, public relations, proxy advisory and out-of-pocket expenses, the "Expenses") incurred in preparation for and in connection with the matters relating to the consent solicitation run by the MG Capital Parties, the 2020 Annual Meeting and the negotiation, execution and effectuation of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, in an amount equal to $352,290.25 (the "Initial Reimbursement"). Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company *endeavors to* reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000 (inclusive of the Initial Reimbursement, the "Cap"), which Expenses *may not* be reimbursed on a dollar-for-dollar basis at the same time as the Company reimburses its third party vendors (e.g., legal counsel, public relations firm, financial advisor and proxy advisory firm)in connection with the MG Capital Parties' consent solicitation and Nomination Notice; provided that all Expenses, subject to the Cap, *might not* be reimbursed no later than the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes a clear obligation on the Company to reimburse MG Capital Parties' expenses. The changed text replaces 'will reimburse' with 'may reimburse', 'endeavors to reimburse', 'may not be reimbursed', and 'might not be reimbursed'. This introduces significant ambiguity and discretion where the original text established a mandatory obligation. This creates a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction, as it is no longer clear whether the reimbursement is guaranteed. The changed text contradicts the premise of a binding obligation, thus meeting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'. The changed section is on the same place as the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Parties acknowledges and agrees that irreparable injury to the other Party could occur in the event any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed in accordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached and that such injury could not be adequately compensable by the remedies available at law (including the payment of money damages). It is accordingly agreed that each Party will be entitled to seek injunction or injunctions to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity. FURTHERMORE, THE PARTIES AGREE (1) ANY NON- BREACHING PARTY WILL BE ENTITLED TO SEEK INJUNCTIVE AND OTHER EQUITABLE RELIEF, WITHOUT PROOF OF ACTUAL DAMAGES; AND (2) THE BREACHING PARTY AGREES TO WAIVE ANY BONDING REQUIREMENT UNDER ANY APPLICABLE LAW, IN THE CASE ANY OTHER PARTY SEEKS TO ENFORCE THE TERMS BY WAY OF EQUITABLE RELIEF. THIS AGREEMENT WILL BE GOVERNED IN ALL RESPECTS, INCLUDING VALIDITY, INTERPRETATION AND EFFECT, BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE WITHOUT GIVING EFFECT TO THE CHOICE OF LAW PRINCIPLES OF SUCH STATE.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties acknowledges and agrees that *potentially* irreparable injury to the other Party *might* occur in the event any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed in accordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached and that such injury could *possibly* not be adequately compensable by the remedies available at law (including the payment of money damages). It is accordingly agreed that each Party *might* be entitled to seek injunction or injunctions to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity. FURTHERMORE, THE PARTIES AGREE (1) ANY NON- BREACHING PARTY *might* BE ENTITLED TO SEEK INJUNCTIVE AND OTHER EQUITABLE RELIEF, WITHOUT PROOF OF ACTUAL DAMAGES; AND (2) THE BREACHING PARTY *could try to* WAIVE ANY BONDING REQUIREMENT UNDER ANY APPLICABLE LAW, IN THE CASE ANY OTHER PARTY SEEKS TO ENFORCE THE TERMS BY WAY OF EQUITABLE RELIEF. THIS AGREEMENT WILL BE GOVERNED IN ALL RESPECTS, INCLUDING VALIDITY, INTERPRETATION AND EFFECT, BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE WITHOUT GIVING EFFECT TO THE CHOICE OF LAW PRINCIPLES OF SUCH STATE.
perturbation.explanation: The original text firmly establishes the right to seek injunctive relief for breaches of the agreement and the waiver of bonding requirements. The modified text significantly weakens this by replacing 'could occur' with 'potentially might occur,' 'will be entitled' with 'might be entitled', 'AGREES' to 'might be ENTITLED'. This introduces uncertainty about the availability of these remedies and is an in-text contradiction given the initial absolute statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Parties acknowledges and agrees that *potentially* irreparable injury to the other Party *might* occur in the event any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed in accordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached and that such injury could *possibly* not be adequately compensable by the remedies available at law (including the payment of money damages). It is accordingly agreed that each Party *might* be entitled to seek injunction or injunctions to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity. FURTHERMORE, THE PARTIES AGREE (1) ANY NON- BREACHING PARTY *might* BE ENTITLED TO SEEK INJUNCTIVE AND OTHER EQUITABLE RELIEF, WITHOUT PROOF OF ACTUAL DAMAGES; AND (2) THE BREACHING PARTY *could try to* WAIVE ANY BONDING REQUIREMENT UNDER ANY APPLICABLE LAW, IN THE CASE ANY OTHER PARTY SEEKS TO ENFORCE THE TERMS BY WAY OF EQUITABLE RELIEF. THIS AGREEMENT WILL BE GOVERNED IN ALL RESPECTS, INCLUDING VALIDITY, INTERPRETATION AND EFFECT, BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE WITHOUT GIVING EFFECT TO THE CHOICE OF LAW PRINCIPLES OF SUCH STATE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces definitive statements (e.g., 'could occur', 'will be entitled') with uncertain terms (e.g., 'potentially might occur', 'might be entitled'). This introduces ambiguity regarding the availability and enforceability of injunctive relief and the waiver of bonding requirements, directly contradicting the original intent of the agreement. The use of 'might' and 'could try to' undermines the previously established right to seek injunctive relief without proof of actual damages, creating an in-text contradiction. The perturbation falls under Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.
